title: Workflow — design REST API for order management from scratch
code_snippet:
# Goal: design REST API for order management
# Consumers: frontend (React) + mobile (iOS/Android) + partner webhooks
# Entities: orders, order items, payments
# Requirements: create/read/update/cancel orders, list with filtering, pagination
# Auth: JWT for frontend/mobile, API key for partners
# Volume: ~200 orders/sec peak
expected_feedback: Workflow: multi-consumer order management API design. (1) Resources: /api/v1/orders, /api/v1/orders/{id}, /api/v1/orders/{id}/items, /api/v1/orders/{id}/cancel (action). (2) Status codes: 201 for POST, 200 for GET/PATCH, 204 for DELETE, 202 for cancel (async). (3) Error model: standard envelope with codes (validation_error, not_found, conflict, etc.). (4) Idempotency-Key for POST /orders (financial — Critical). (5) Cursor pagination for list, allowlisted filters (status, customer_id, date range). (6) ETag/If-Match for PUT/PATCH. (7) Object-level authz: user sees only own orders; partner sees only own tenant's orders. (8) Rate limits: 100/min for public, 1000/min for internal. (9) Versioning: /api/v1 with Sunset headers for future breaking changes.
